overview: Grixis Tempo in Legacy leverages efficient early pressure and superior card selection to outmaneuver opponents. By combining cheap threats with proactive draw and protection, the deck maintains board presence while disrupting enemy plans. The goal is to dictate the pace of the game and close it before opponents can stabilize.
win_conditions: Attacking with a countered Delver of Secrets
Wearing down opponents through efficient removal and card advantage
Closing out games with Flow State-empowered threats

keep_criteria: Hands featuring Ponder alongside a one-drop or early interaction
Playable curves that include Flow State or Delver of Secrets
Balanced mana bases with at least two lands and a threat
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (including fetches/shocks), Ponder, early disruption (Thoughtseize/Inquisition), and Delver/Flow State.
Prioritize hands that can Ponder into a land or cast disruption on turn one.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early plays or interactive spells
Multiple high-cost cards without sufficient mana sources
Unplayable land counts or color screw risks
Mulligan on 3+ lands with no spells, 0-1 lands with no cantrips, or hands lacking a turn-1 play.

early_game: Cast Ponder to optimize your opening hand and find lands or answers
Play Delver of Secrets and counter it to establish a 4/2 flying threat
Use cheap interaction to clear opposing board states and maintain tempo
Turn 1: Ponder into a land or cast Thoughtseize/Inquisition on a key threat or combo piece.
Turn 2: Drop Delver if it's a two-drop, or cast Flow State if ahead, or play a shock land/mana source.
Sequence mana carefully to keep options open and avoid tapping out unnecessarily.
mid_game: Deploy Flow State to protect key threats and draw into additional resources
Trade efficiently to keep pressure on while digging for finishers
Maintain board presence by replaying countered creatures or leveraging card advantage
Use Ponder to find lands or key spells
Manage your graveyard carefully—Flow State needs it, but be wary of hate.
Keep hand size up with cantrips and discard to maintain card advantage.
Commit Delver when you have removal for their threats or they're tapped out.
Hold back if they have sweepers or counters.
late_game: Leverage accumulated card advantage to outlast opponent's resources
Use remaining interaction to shut down comeback attempts
Close the game with a resilient board state that opponents cannot easily clear
Delver of Secrets (Aetherborn), Flow State, efficient removal, and card advantage engines.
Win by out-resourcing and closing with a lethal threat or combo.
Go all-in when you have card advantage, they're tapped out, or you've removed their key answers.
Commit with Delver + Flow State + removal to close the game.

key_cards.name: Delver of Secrets
key_cards.notes: Counter it on turn two to create a 4/2 flying creature that pressures opponents immediately and forces unfavorable trades.

key_cards.name: Flow State
key_cards.notes: Use to untap and draw cards when you need to replay threats or survive a combat, ensuring you maintain board control and tempo.

key_cards.name: Ponder
key_cards.notes: Play on your own turn to find lands, removal, or threats, and always consider mulligging to improve your next turn's options.

sequencing_tips: Always Ponder before committing to a play to ensure you have the right answer or threat
Counter Delver only when you are confident you can follow up with pressure or interaction
Use Flow State reactively to protect against sweepers or combat tricks while maintaining initiative
Common mistakes include overcommitting to the board, mismanaging mana, ignoring graveyard hate, and Pondering into dead cards.
Unique interactions: Flow State synergy with Delver, Ponder for consistency, and disruption timing.
Always track their graveyard and hand size to predict their moves.

matchups.advice: Prioritize early interaction and keep Delver on the board to force trades and stall their clock.
Apply consistent pressure and use Flow State to bypass counterspells and recover from board wipes.
Disrupt their setup with early interaction and maintain a threatening board state to force them to respond.
Use Ponder to dig for answers, cast threats at end step, or chain multiple threats. Flow State can bypass counters if cast from the graveyard. Sideboard in cheaper or uncounterable threats if needed.
Early disruption is key. Thoughtseize/Inquisition to hit mana dorks or key rocks. Spot removal on ramp threats. Don't overextend; out-tempo them with efficient plays.
Win the disruption war. Out-Ponder, out-discard, and out-tempo. Sideboard for more counters and disruption. Track their hand and graveyard to predict their plays.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (Wrath, Damnation), counterspells, graveyard hate, and combo pieces.
Note when they tap out for big spells or when they're low on resources.
Track their graveyard for Flow State or Sneak and Show threats.
